metadata.title: Don't Ship the Most Accurate Model

beats.narration_text: Aloha, Liam. This is Liam, in for Bear. The best model on the leaderboard failed on the device by four kilobytes; the one that shipped was worse on every benchmark.

props.command: Which model should I ship? The leaderboard winner doesn't fit my flash budget by 40 KB.
output: Strike infeasible candidates. Eliminate dominated ones. Ship the surviving Pareto-optimal point with margin.

beats.narration_text: The book's Pareto scatter: six face-detection candidates on latency versus accuracy. Two are dominated — strictly worse on both axes. The frontier is the only honest shortlist.

A scatter of model candidates: accuracy on the vertical axis, flash size on the horizontal. The leaderboard winner: 94% accuracy, 2.04 megabytes. It's a terracotta dot past the flash ceiling. 2 megabytes is your limit. The winner is 40 kilobytes over. It doesn't matter that it's the best — it can't ship.

The constraints: flash ceiling at 2 megabytes, latency budget at 50 milliseconds. Lines close in. Every dot above the flash ceiling is out. Every dot past the latency budget is out. A cluster of candidates survives — not the most accurate, but feasible. These are the only models that can actually deploy.

Among feasible candidates: the Pareto frontier. Each point on it is not dominated — no other candidate is better on both accuracy and flash simultaneously. Dominated models grey out. The frontier traces itself. The shipped model: 91% accuracy, 1.8 megabytes — 200 kilobytes of margin inside the 2-megabyte ceiling. Three percent less accurate than the leaderboard winner. Ships every time.

Your turn. Ask Claude: I have these model candidates with accuracy, latency in milliseconds, and flash in kilobytes — and list them. My constraints are open bracket max latency close bracket ms and open bracket max flash close bracket KB. Walk me through: one — which candidates are infeasible. Two — which feasible ones are dominated. Three — the Pareto frontier among feasible candidates. Four — how to choose among Pareto-optimal candidates with appropriate margin.

props.command: I have these model candidates with (accuracy, latency_ms, flash_kb): [list]. My constraints are [max_latency] ms and [max_flash] KB. Walk me through: (1) which candidates are infeasible, (2) which feasible ones are dominated, (3) the Pareto frontier among feasible candidates, (4) how to choose among Pareto-optimal candidates with appropriate margin.
output: Best model: 94% accuracy, 2.04 MB — 40 KB over limit. Shipped model: 91%, 1.8 MB — 200 KB margin. Worse benchmark, ships reliably.

props.title: Don't Ship the Most Accurate Model
props.subline: embedded ai · liam, in for bear
